title: HighpassFilter
description: High-pass filter backed by a `BiquadFilterNode` in `highpass` mode. Attenuates frequencies below the cutoff and passes everything above it. Useful for removing low-frequency rumble, thinning out bass-heavy audio, or sculpting mix headroom. The `resonance` (Q) parameter controls the sharpness of the roll-off peak at the cutoff frequency.

sections.title: Import
paragraphs: High-pass filter backed by a `BiquadFilterNode` in `highpass` mode. Attenuates frequencies below the cutoff and passes everything above it. Useful for removing low-frequency rumble, thinning out bass-heavy audio, or sculpting mix headroom. The `resonance` (Q) parameter controls the sharpness of the roll-off peak at the cutoff frequency.

members.name: destroy
members.description: Disconnects all audio nodes and releases resources. Must be called when the effect is no longer needed.

members.name: frequency
members.description: Cutoff frequency in Hz. Signals below this frequency are attenuated. Range 20..20000, default 1000.

members.name: inputNode
members.description: The node where audio enters this effect.

members.name: outputNode
members.description: The node where audio exits this effect.

members.name: ready
members.description: Resolves when the effect is fully initialized. Defaults to immediately-resolved for sync effects.

members.name: resonance
members.description: Q (resonance) at the cutoff. Higher values create a sharper, resonant peak. Minimum 0.0001, default 1.
